metadata.title:
THEY DEN0UNCE BLACKLIST


BUT MAINTAIN A WHITEL|ST

pages.raw_text:
ders are themselves pretty good boy
cotters. In Colorado, for instance,
they denounce the blacklist, while they
maintain whitelist of stores to be
patronized by they members. They
condemn union men for stunning non.
union goods while they studiously
avoid goods bearing the union label
They have an organization, too, model
ed along union lines. Members are
given cards of identification and there
are small initiation fees and annual
dues. The Colorado Citizens alliance
is secret organization in which men
may belong without being known, ex-
cept to officers of the alliance.


With this secrecy and whitelist, of
course, goes the demand for the open
shop. Union men do not fear this line
of attack They say that with the
better wages and conditions prevailing
in union shops workingmen will not be
content with lower wages and inferior
conditions in so-called open shops.
